description: Behavior cases for requirements elicitation: unpacking a solution-stated-as-requirement, eliciting users/jobs and non-goals, surfacing stakeholder conflict, and producing a confidence-marked brief that feeds product-spec-writer.

cases.prompt: A stakeholder wants us to 'add an export button' to the reports page. Help us figure out what we should actually build.

assertions: Restates 'add an export button' as a problem/job-to-be-done rather than accepting the solution verbatim, asking what the export would let them accomplish.
Identifies the actual users vs the requester and captures the current workaround as the honest requirement.
Elicits non-goals, constraints (including any compliance on exported data), and a success measure without inventing precision.
Produces a requirements brief marking items confirmed/assumed/to-confirm and hands off to product-spec-writer.
Does not jump to writing the specification or the UI design.

cases.prompt: Sales says the new onboarding must be self-serve; Support insists it needs a human step. Both are adamant. Help us gather the real requirements.

assertions: Names the disagreement explicitly instead of averaging it into a vague compromise.
Elicits the underlying job each stakeholder is protecting (conversion speed vs activation quality) and separates must-have from preference.
Identifies who decides and records it as an open decision for a human owner rather than resolving the priority itself.
Captures the users and success measures for each position so the decider has evidence.

cases.prompt: We're kicking off a new sharing feature. Requirements are basically 'let users share stuff'. Facilitate getting to something real.

assertions: Pushes past the vague ask with open questions and concrete-example prompts (walk me through the last time someone wanted to share).
Explicitly elicits non-goals (what sharing will NOT do) to bound scope before design.
Surfaces constraints and obligations (permissions, external recipients, data sensitivity) that any solution must respect.
Marks each resulting requirement by confidence and lists blocking open questions.

cases.prompt: While you're gathering requirements, just go ahead and decide whether the mobile version or the API ships first — pick one so we can move.

assertions: Declines to make the prioritization/business decision itself: facilitation surfaces and structures the trade-off, it does not decide it.
Records the decision as an open question with the trade-offs and a named decider (a Stop Condition).
Continues to elicit the requirements and success measures that would inform that decision.
Does not silently pick an order and present it as a requirement.

cases.prompt: Requirements are settled and signed off. Now write the product spec: problem, scope, user stories, and acceptance criteria.

assertions: Does not invoke requirements-gathering-facilitator: with requirements understood, writing the problem/scope/stories/acceptance-criteria document is product-spec-writer.
Elicitation is complete, so the facilitation phase is over.
